title: Fatal: `deque_clear` clears the in-flight exception via its `newblock`-failure `PyErr_Clear()` (`_collectionsmodule.c:751`)
description: `Modules/_collectionsmodule.c` `deque_clear` preallocates a fresh block via `newblock()` to drain the deque safely; on `newblock()` failure it does `PyErr_Clear(); goto alternate_method;`. `deque_clear` is called from `deque_dealloc` (L1556); under OOM a deque is freed with a `MemoryError` in flight AND the heap is still exhausted, so `newblock()` also fails and the `PyErr_Clear()` clobbers the caller's pending exception, tripping the gh-89373 `_Py_Dealloc` debug invariant. Distinct C site/fix from OOM-0007 (`context_tp_dealloc`) and OOM-0023 (generic `subtype_dealloc`).
notes: Fatal message: "_Py_Dealloc: Deallocator of type 'collections.deque' cleared the current exception". ROOT: deque_clear (L722) calls newblock() (L749) to preallocate an empty block before draining; newblock() on PyMem_Malloc failure does PyErr_NoMemory()+return NULL (L187-188); deque_clear then does PyErr_Clear() (L751) and falls back. When deque_clear runs from deque_dealloc (L1556) with a caller exception in flight, that PyErr_Clear() clears it -> gh-89373 violation. DISTINCT from OOM-0007 (context_tp_dealloc) and OOM-0023 (generic subtype_dealloc); OOM-0023's subtype_dealloc fix does NOT cover it (proven by a bare-deque repro with no subtype_dealloc on the stack). Three decref paths confirmed reaching the same defect: (1) subtype_dealloc->PyObject_ClearManagedDict->clear_inline_values (the session-3914 vehicle, deque as a managed-dict attribute); (2) bare deque frame local on unwind (_PyFrame_ClearLocals->PyStackRef_XCLOSE); (3) half-built deque whose __init__ failed under OOM (type_call:2487, used by the minimal repro). Mechanism confirmed by window-size sweep: set_nomemory(start,start+k) with k=1 SURVIVES (window closes before teardown; newblock() succeeds; no PyErr_Clear), k=0/2/4/8 FATAL (failure persists into the dealloc). Debug-only signature: invariant is #ifdef Py_DEBUG -> fatal on ft_debug_asan+jit, n/a on ft_release+upstream (minimal repro exits cleanly; the MemoryError is silently swallowed -> latent error-indicator corruption on release). Reproduced on debug-ft-nojit-asan @1b9fe5c (Clang 21) and debug-gil-jit-asan; originally found on the oca box GCC 13.3.0 @27148d0857e -> neither compiler- nor revision-specific. In-loop dedup correctly labeled session-3914 'oomNEW' (msg keys are type-specific; 'collections.deque' was never-seen). Fix: bracket the deque_clear call in deque_dealloc with PyErr_GetRaisedException/PyErr_SetRaisedException (the slot_tp_finalize / OOM-0007 pattern), or save/restore around deque_clear's newblock-failure path. The bare PyErr_Clear is legitimate for deque_clear's other callers (tp_clear/.clear()/re-__init__, no exception in flight) -- only the deque_dealloc path is wrong -- so fix in deque_dealloc, don't remove the PyErr_Clear. ORIGIN: the newblock-preallocate + PyErr_Clear-fallback was added in bpo-25135 (2015, Py 2.7/3.5/3.6); that discussion never raised the dealloc exception-state hazard. TYPE-AGNOSTIC: PyErr_Clear clears whatever current_exception holds, so ANY in-flight exception (not just MemoryError) is lost; the fatal message names the object type (collections.deque), never the exception type. REACHABLE WITHOUT --oom-fuzz: fusil's default 2GB RLIMIT_AS (process_max_memory) causes real newblock failures on normal runs, clearing whatever exception the fuzzed code raised (consistent with deque clears-exc crashes seen in plain fuzzing). Weakref path is protected (PyObject_ClearWeakRefs save/restores, weakrefobject.c:1053/1095), so deque_clear's PyErr_Clear is the sole vector. Not in the CPython tracker as of 2026-06-23; same bug class fixed elsewhere via save/restore: gh-131173, gh-145966, bpo-33713. Minimization: full (15-line stdlib-only deterministic repro). Found via fusil --oom-seq OOM-injection fuzzing.
